judge_reason: Description B misquotes the Greyhound caption as 'Peeved with Parking?' while Description A quotes it as 'Parked with Parking?', and it describes the main bus visual as a 'line sketch of a bus interior' rather than the stylized illustration of people in a waiting area described by A.
original_image_tags: Color advertisement for King Blended Whisky by Brown-Forman. Shows a man sitting in an armchair holding a highball glass, a bottle of King whisky, and a highball glass with ice and a cherry. Text reads "Looks Lighter! Tastes Lighter!" and "Today's KING is Really LIGHT!".

Color illustration for Greyhound bus travel. Shows several groups of people in a stylized bus or waiting area: a honeymoon couple, a businessman, a housewife sightseeing, and a college student. A small inset shows a bus with the text "Parked with Parking? Take a Greyhound!". The main headline reads "Relax - as you See - as you Save by Greyhound!".
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A two-page magazine spread with multiple color advertisements and article text: at left, a Brown-Forman “King” blended whisky ad with a whiskey bottle, a glass, and a seated man illustration; at right, a Greyhound bus travel ad with several illustrated people, a Greyhound bus, and the headline “Relax – as you See – as you Save by Greyhound!”*
